title: boronia pilosa subsp. tasmanensis
intro: Boronia pilosa subsp. tasmanensis is a plant species in the Boraginaceae family, specifically a subspecies of the Boronia pilosa, native to Tasmania, Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Boronia pilosa subsp. tasmanensis include reducing inflammation and relieving pain, making it useful for treating various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are often used in aromatherapy and topical applications to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
